question_text: The product of all eigen values of the matrix $ \left[ { \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 4 } & { 5 } & { 6 } \\ { 7 } & { 8 } & { 9 } \end{array} } \right] $
solution:
To determine the product of all eigenvalues of the matrix \( A = \left[ \begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 & 9 \end{array} \right] \), we can use the property that the product of the eigenvalues of a matrix is equal to its determinant. Therefore, we need to compute the determinant of \( A \).

The determinant of a 3x3 matrix \( \left[ \begin{array}{ccc} a & b & c \\ d & e & f \\ g & h & i \end{array} \right] \) is given by the formula:
\[
\det(A) = a(ei - fh) - b(di - fg) + c(dh - eg)
\]
For the matrix \( A = \left[ \begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 4 & 5 & 6 \\ 7 & 8 & 9 \end{array} \right] \), we substitute \( a = 1 \), \( b = 2 \), \( c = 3 \), \( d = 4 \), \( e = 5 \), \( f = 6 \), \( g = 7 \), \( h = 8 \), and \( i = 9 \) into the formula:
\[
\det(A) = 1(5 \cdot 9 - 6 \cdot 8) - 2(4 \cdot 9 - 6 \cdot 7) + 3(4 \cdot 8 - 5 \cdot 7)
\]
First, we calculate the individual products:
\[
5 \cdot 9 = 45, \quad 6 \cdot 8 = 48, \quad 4 \cdot 9 = 36, \quad 6 \cdot 7 = 42, \quad 4 \cdot 8 = 32, \quad 5 \cdot 7 = 35
\]
Next, we substitute these values back into the determinant formula:
\[
\det(A) = 1(45 - 48) - 2(36 - 42) + 3(32 - 35)
\]
Simplify the expressions inside the parentheses:
\[
\det(A) = 1(-3) - 2(-6) + 3(-3)
\]
Then, perform the multiplications:
\[
\det(A) = -3 + 12 - 9
\]
Finally, add the results together:
\[
\det(A) = 0
\]
Since the determinant of the matrix is 0, the product of all eigenvalues of the matrix is also 0. Therefore, the correct answer is:
\[
\boxed{A}
\]
